title: RIID-4881 / RIID-4917: Codex Auth And Full-Access Harness

blocks.text: The initial task-scoped `CODEX_HOME` model prevented Codex from reading the user's global config and removed Codex-managed ChatGPT auth. Real SaaS assignments then failed with provider 401 responses.

RIID-4881 first kept the real Codex auth store available while trying a daemon-owned permission profile for provider tool commands. RIID-4917 supersedes that experiment: daemon no longer owns Codex internal filesystem permission semantics.

items: C6 no longer materializes Codex `.codex/config.toml` or `native_config_home=<workdir>/.codex`.
`danger-full-access` is the only Codex sandbox selection generated by daemon.
Free-form custom args cannot pass Codex protocol-critical config, sandbox, yolo, or danger-bypass flags.
Harness owns assignment snapshot, daemon-selected workdir/evidence root, lifecycle, heartbeat/stale lease handling, terminal result reporting, dropped arg evidence, and provider integration gates.
Codex runtime registration may report host Codex config `model` as a runtime-scoped model catalog entry instead of inventing `codex-default`.

blocks.text: `docs/20-domain/security.md` owns the C7 judgment that full-access/trusted runtime envelopes are explicit harness-managed choices. `docs/20-domain/provider-runtime.md` owns provider-by-provider C4 adoption.

This slice does not move OpenClaw/Cursor adapters, supervisor polling/runtime selection, SaaS control-plane adapters, task DB/project/mwsd local API packages, packaging artifacts, private infra, secrets, or local machine state.
